title: Droidbreaker
content:

{{Quote|There are dangers on Belgaroth ''far'' worse than your battle droids.|[[Tarin (Human)|Tarin]], to [[Reep Tonith]]|<ref name="ATT" />}}
The '''droidbreaker''', formerly known as the '''swamphulk''', was a creature native to [[Belgaroth]]. Its native planet consumed by pollution and scrapyards, the droidbreaker evolved in a single generation to survive and fit its new surroundings. With a steady diet of [[Plasteel/Legends|plasteel]] and different forms of refined metal, the droidbreaker became a hulking figure that skulked the many junkyards that covered the planet. A [[droidbreaker steak]] could be made from droidbreaker meat and was high in iron content due to the droidbreaker's diet.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Before we've even finished loading the shipment, the plasteel-loving ''droidbreaker'' will have chewed the armor from their backs! There is no escaping the ''swamphulk''!|[[Tarin (Human)|Tarin]]|<ref name="ATT" />}}
These creatures reached five [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall at the shoulders. They moved on four short [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and were covered in [[Color/Legends|gray]], leathery hide. Small black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] sat deep in their [[Bone/Legends|skulls]], while a triangular head,<ref name="CW" /> crowned with a bright [[Exoskeleton|carapace]],<ref name="ATT" /> ended in an elongated bony proboscis. Droidbreakers used their strong, sharp proboscises to pierce [[Armor/Legends|armor]], [[Metal/Legends|metallic]] plating, and the thick hides of other beings,<ref name="CW" /> in order to grind them up immediately with rows of sharpened [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]].<ref name="ATT" /> To seize prey, they employed an extremely strong, slippery gray [[Tongue/Legends|tongue]].<ref name="CW" />

[[File:SwamphulkOnBelgaroth.jpg|left|thumb|280px|Swamphulk in its natural habitat]]
Originally, the predatory droidbreakers inhabited [[Swamp/Legends|swampy]] regions, which earned them another, no less well-known name – swamphulk. Even in early times, they hunted various creatures and careless travelers, instilling fear in the inhabitants of [[Belgaroth]]. In addition to their [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] preferences, these beings also consumed [[Plasteel/Legends|plasteel]], seeking out [[Droid/Legends|droids]] or victims equipped with [[Armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="ATT" />

Possessing a high adaptability to changes in their environment, droidbreakers managed to restructure their biochemistry within a single generation in order to survive the ecological collapse of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. They developed immunity to [[Poison/Legends|poisons]], resistance to [[Acid/Legends|acids]], and drastically altered their diet, abandoning their originally carnivorous way of feeding. The basis of their new sustenance became plasteel and processed metals, which droidbreakers could assimilate without harm. They particularly favored wiring and circuits, which made functioning droids a desirable prey. Having evolved the ability to sense active energy sources through their emissions, droidbreakers were capable of detecting working power cells from up to 200 meters away. In addition, by means of electromagnetic waves, they could determine the location of metallic objects within a radius of 10 meters.<ref name="CW" />

==History==
{{Quote|Not droids… something else… something big… something very big — and approaching at speed!|[[Recon]] senses the approach of the droidbreaker|<ref name="ATT" />}}
[[File:FEX-M3 grenade detonated.jpg|right|thumb||280px|The droidbreaker dies from a nerve gas grenade]]
During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a squad of four clone scouts of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] landed on [[Belgaroth]] to investigate a report that the [[Czerka factory|Czerka weapons factory]] was trading with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]. Infiltrating the enemy facility, they found a shipment of prohibited [[FEX-M3 nerve gas grenade]]s. [[Marrt]], one of the soldiers, took a grenade from the crate as evidence of the factory’s criminal activities. During their retreat, the squad was spotted by [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1 battle droids]] who, under the command of [[Tarin (Human)|Tarin]], drove the clones into a misty [[Swamp/Legends|swamp]] inhabited by deadly swamphulks. The clones believed they had shaken off their pursuers, but [[Recon]], one of them, sensed the approach of a huge unknown entity. Suddenly, a swamphulk burst out of the fog and attacked the scouts. The creature quickly killed all but Marrt, who devised a way to deal with it. He threw the FEX-M3 grenade toward the swamphulk, hoping it would catch it, and fired at it with his pistol. Nearly all of the shots ricocheted off the creature’s head carapace, but one struck the grenade directly, killing the monster with nerve gas. Marrt buried his comrades and placed the head of the swamphulk above their graves.<ref name="ATT" />

By the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the planet Belgaroth became a classic example of a small, insignificant world ruined by the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] for its own selfish purposes. The ecological catastrophe caused major changes in the planet’s natural environment and forced local species of flora and fauna to adapt rapidly to the alterations.<ref name="CW" /> The droidbreakers, who had previously not shunned inorganic materials,<ref name="ATT" /> easily adapted to digesting aggressive chemicals and consuming processed metallic waste. In the altered world, wild droidbreakers populated the surroundings of the so-called [[Scrapyard (Belgaroth)|Scrapyard]], where numerous expended weapons, burned-out droids, and toxic waste were discarded. Conditions in the Scrapyard were terrible, but the droidbreakers learned how to survive there.<ref name="CW" />

Some [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that fed on [[Iron/Legends|iron]]-containing food considered droidbreaker meat a true delicacy: a single portion [[Droidbreaker steak|steak]] would cost no less than 200 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="CW" />

==Behind the scenes==
The droidbreaker were designed for the sourcebook ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' {{C|[[2003]]}} for the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]] by [[Wizards of the Coast]]. The information section about the planet [[Belgaroth]] offered game scenarios set on this world, among them the story «A-Hunting We Will Go». In the plot, a local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who desired a steak made from droidbreaker meat forced the player characters to go hunting in the habitat of wild droidbreaker in exchange for the return of their confiscated [[Starship/Legends|ship]].
